name: Dover
summary: Dover is a seaside town in southern England famous for its towering White Cliffs of soft chalk and a huge medieval castle once nicknamed the 'Key to England' that watches over the narrowest part of the Channel.
funFacts: The White Cliffs are made of chalk built up from billions of tiny ancient sea creatures, and you can still spot fossils like sea urchins poking out of them.
Dover Castle has secret tunnels carved into the cliffs that helped rescue 338,000 soldiers from Dunkirk during World War Two.
The Roman Pharos lighthouse inside Dover Castle is almost 2,000 years old and is one of only three Roman lighthouses still standing in the world.
South Foreland Lighthouse near Dover was the very first lighthouse anywhere to shine using electricity, way back in 1858.
Dover is the closest place in Britain to France, with the cliffs of Calais just 21 miles away across the Channel on a clear day.
thingsToDo: Walk the springy clifftop path along the famous White Cliffs and look out for ferries crossing to France.
Explore Dover Castle's mighty Great Tower and crawl through the Secret Wartime Tunnels carved into the chalk.
Climb up to the Roman Pharos beside the castle, the tallest Roman building still standing in Britain.
Visit South Foreland Lighthouse and learn how its electric light first warned ships away from the rocks.
Hike up to the Western Heights to see the Drop Redoubt, a star-shaped fort built to scare off Napoleon.
Take a short train ride to Canterbury to see the giant medieval cathedral with its sky-high stone tower.

wildlife.name: Atlantic Puffin
wildlife.description: Chubby black-and-white seabird with a bright orange beak that nests on cliffs near the Channel.

wildlife.name: Peregrine Falcon
wildlife.description: Speedy hunting bird that nests on the White Cliffs and dives faster than a racing car.

wildlife.name: Adonis Blue Butterfly
wildlife.description: Tiny dazzling sky-blue butterfly that flutters across the chalk grasslands above the cliffs.

wildlife.name: Harbour Porpoise
wildlife.description: Small shy cousin of the dolphin that swims and puffs in the cold Channel waters.

localWord.meaning: British way of saying 'awesome' or 'fantastic' when you love something.

images.alt: The famous towering white chalk cliffs of Dover meeting the blue sea on a sunny day.
A bird's-eye view of mighty Dover Castle with its stone walls perched high above the coast.
Henry II's Great Tower, the huge stone keep at the heart of Dover Castle.
The ancient Roman Pharos lighthouse beside the church of St Mary in Castro at Dover Castle.
The white tower of South Foreland Lighthouse standing on the clifftops near Dover.
Grassy ramparts of the Drop Redoubt fort on the Western Heights above Dover.
The west front and central tower of Canterbury Cathedral, a short train ride from Dover.
An Atlantic puffin with its colourful orange beak standing on a grassy cliff.
A peregrine falcon, the world's fastest bird, perched and watching for prey.
A bright sky-blue male Adonis blue butterfly resting on a flower.
A harbour porpoise surfacing in the sea, showing its small dark fin.
